title: Quiz: Advanced Bra-Ket Operations
description: Test your understanding of operator sandwiches, traces, and commutators

questions.question: What is ⟨0|Z|0⟩ where Z is the Pauli Z matrix?
What is Tr(|0⟩⟨1|)?
questions.explanation: Tr(|ψ⟩⟨φ|) = ⟨φ|ψ⟩. So Tr(|0⟩⟨1|) = ⟨1|0⟩ = 0. Both c and d equal 0, but the formula gives ⟨1|0⟩.

questions.question: What is [X, X] (commutator of X with itself)?
questions.explanation: [X, X] = XX - XX = 0. Any operator commutes with itself.

questions.question: Tr(AB) = Tr(BA) for any operators A and B.
questions.explanation: True! This is the cyclic property of trace. Note that AB ≠ BA in general, but their traces are equal.

questions.question: What is [X, Y] for Pauli matrices?
questions.explanation: The Pauli commutation relation: [X, Y] = XY - YX = 2iZ.

questions.question: For the Bell state |Φ+⟩ = (|00⟩+|11⟩)/√2, what is TrB(|Φ+⟩⟨Φ+|)?
questions.explanation: Tracing out B from a maximally entangled Bell state gives the maximally mixed state I/2.

questions.question: ⟨ab|cd⟩ = ⟨a|c⟩ · ⟨b|d⟩ for tensor product states.
questions.explanation: True! The inner product of tensor products factors: ⟨a⊗b|c⊗d⟩ = ⟨a|c⟩⟨b|d⟩.

questions.question: Which of the following are correct? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All are correct! a) and b) are linearity. c) is cyclic property (Tr(ABC)=Tr(BCA)=Tr(CAB)). d) Tr(I) = n for n×n identity.
